title: Jeanne Dielman, 23 quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles
desc: Three days in the life of a Brussels housewife. The greatest film ever made according to Sight & Sound 2022.

title: Vertigo
desc: Hitchcock's most personal film. A retired detective becomes obsessed with a woman he is hired to follow.

title: Citizen Kane
desc: The rise and fall of a media magnate. Formally revolutionary and still stunning.

title: Tokyo Story
desc: Elderly parents visit their adult children in Tokyo. Devastating in its quietness.

title: In the Mood for Love
desc: Two neighbours in 1962 Hong Kong suspect their spouses are having an affair. Exquisitely made.

title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
desc: Humanity's evolution from apes to astronauts. The monolith. HAL. Still unmatched.

title: Beau Travail
desc: Foreign Legion soldiers in Djibouti. Denis at her most hypnotic.

title: Mulholland Drive
desc: An aspiring actress and an amnesiac woman investigate a mystery in Hollywood.

title: Singin' in the Rain
desc: Hollywood transitions to talking pictures. The best musical ever made.

title: Man with a Movie Camera
desc: A day in Soviet cities, edited into a manifesto for pure cinema.

title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
desc: A farmer is tempted to murder his wife. One of the most beautiful films ever shot.

title: The Passion of Joan of Arc
desc: Joan's trial and execution in close-up. Maria Falconetti's face. Nothing else like it.

title: Rules of the Game
desc: A weekend house party exposes the rot of French bourgeois society.

title: Battleship Potemkin
desc: The 1905 mutiny and the Odessa Steps sequence. The film that invented editing.

title: L'Atalante
desc: Newlyweds on a canal barge. Romantic, surreal, heartbreaking.

title: The Red Shoes
desc: A ballerina torn between love and art. Overwhelming Technicolor. One of the great British films.

title: A Matter of Life and Death
desc: A pilot survives a jump without a parachute and is put on trial in heaven.

title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp
desc: A British soldier's career across three wars. Churchill tried to ban it.

title: Black Narcissus
desc: Nuns establish a convent in the Himalayas. Repression and hysteria at altitude.

title: The Tales of Hoffmann
desc: Offenbach's opera filmed as pure cinematic spectacle.

title: L'Avventura
desc: A woman disappears on a Sicilian island. Her companion and lover search for her without urgency.

title: La Dolce Vita
desc: A tabloid journalist drifts through Roman high society. Overwhelming and magnificent.

title: 8½
desc: A film director retreats into fantasy while trying to make a film.

title: Rome, Open City
desc: The Italian Resistance in Nazi-occupied Rome. Shot on the streets immediately after liberation.

title: Bicycle Thieves
desc: A man and his son search Rome for a stolen bicycle. The heart of neorealism.

title: The Leopard
desc: A Sicilian aristocrat navigates the Risorgimento. Burt Lancaster gives the performance of his life.

title: Rocco and His Brothers
desc: A family migrates from the south to Milan. Five brothers, one tragedy.

title: The Gospel According to St. Matthew
desc: Christ's life shot with non-actors in southern Italy. Austere and devastating.

title: Breathless
desc: A small-time thief goes on the run in Paris. Invented modern cinema grammar.

title: The 400 Blows
desc: A misunderstood Parisian boy's descent into delinquency. Autobiographical and heartbreaking.

title: Cleo from 5 to 7
desc: A pop singer awaits the results of a cancer test. Real time. Paris in 1962.

title: Pierrot le Fou
desc: A man leaves his wife and runs away with his babysitter. Genre exploded into primary colours.

title: The Umbrellas of Cherbourg
desc: Every word sung. A love story broken by the Algerian War.

title: Mon Oncle
desc: Tati's Monsieur Hulot struggles with modern suburban life. Gentle, precise, hilarious.

title: Playtime
desc: Hulot lost in a glass-and-steel Paris. The most expensive French film ever made. A masterwork.

title: Traffic
desc: Hulot drives a camper van to an Amsterdam car show. Tati's farewell to cinema.

title: Wild Strawberries
desc: An elderly professor drives to collect an honorary degree and confronts his life.

title: The Seventh Seal
desc: A knight plays chess with Death during the Black Plague.

title: Persona
desc: An actress stops speaking. Her nurse begins to lose herself. Identity dissolves.

title: Scenes from a Marriage
desc: A marriage's disintegration over six episodes. Liv Ullmann and Erland Josephson. Devastating.

title: The Mirror
desc: Memories, dreams, newsreel. Tarkovsky's most personal film.

title: Stalker
desc: A guide leads two men through a forbidden zone to a room that grants wishes.

title: Day of Wrath
desc: A young woman accused of witchcraft in 17th century Denmark. Made under Nazi occupation.

title: Fanny and Alexander
desc: A bourgeois Swedish family at Christmas. Bergman's farewell to cinema. Glorious.

title: Metropolis
desc: A dystopian future city. Workers underground, elites above. Lang's vision is still overwhelming.

title: Nosferatu
desc: The original vampire film. Max Schreck's shadow on the wall.

title: M
desc: A child murderer is hunted by both police and criminals. Lang's masterpiece.

title: The Marriage of Maria Braun
desc: A woman's survival in postwar West Germany. Fassbinder's most accessible film.

title: Paris, Texas
desc: A man emerges from the desert and tries to reconnect with his family.

title: Pather Panchali
desc: A Bengali family in rural poverty. The first of the Apu Trilogy. Ray's debut.

title: Seven Samurai
desc: Seven samurai defend a village. The template for the action film.

title: Rashomon
desc: Four accounts of a murder in a forest. Truth is relative. Still radical.

title: Yi Yi
desc: A Taipei family across three generations over one year. Three hours of perfection.

title: A Brighter Summer Day
desc: A teenager in 1960s Taipei navigates gang violence and first love. Four hours. Magnificent.

title: In the Heat of the Sun
desc: A teenager's summer in Cultural Revolution Beijing. Memory, desire, heat.

title: Close-Up
desc: A man impersonates a famous director and is put on trial. Documentary and fiction merge.

title: A Separation
desc: An Iranian couple's separation becomes a legal and moral crisis. Oscar winner.

title: Parasite
desc: A poor family infiltrates a wealthy household. Genre-defying. Palme d'Or and Best Picture.

title: Memories of Murder
desc: South Korea's first serial killer investigation. One of the great crime films.

title: The Spy Who Came in from the Cold
desc: A burnt-out British agent is sent on one last mission. John le Carré adaptation. The anti-Bond.

title: The Conversation
desc: A surveillance expert becomes paranoid about a recording he has made.

title: Three Days of the Condor
desc: A CIA analyst goes on the run after his colleagues are killed.

title: The Lives of Others
desc: A Stasi officer surveils a playwright in East Germany and begins to change. Oscar winner.

title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy
desc: George Smiley hunts a mole in British intelligence. Gary Oldman. Immaculate.

title: The Day of the Jackal
desc: A professional assassin plans to kill De Gaulle. The procedural thriller at its peak.

title: Come and See
desc: The Nazi massacre of Belarussian villages seen through a boy's eyes. The most devastating war film.

title: The Battle of Algiers
desc: The Algerian independence movement. Shot like a documentary. Still taught at war colleges.

title: Army of Shadows
desc: The French Resistance with no heroics. Melville survived the occupation. This is what it was.

title: Schindler's List
desc: Oskar Schindler saves over a thousand Jewish lives during the Holocaust.

title: The Great Dictator
desc: Chaplin plays both Hitler and a Jewish barber. His speech at the end still lands.

title: Casablanca
desc: Rick's Cafe, Ilsa, and the underground railroad out of Nazi-occupied Morocco.

title: Au Revoir les Enfants
desc: A French Catholic school hides Jewish children during the occupation. Autobiographical.

title: Europa Europa
desc: A Jewish boy survives the war by passing as a member of the Hitler Youth.

title: The Conformist
desc: A man joins Mussolini's secret police and is sent to kill his mentor.

title: Hiroshima Mon Amour
desc: A French actress and a Japanese architect remember their wartime losses.

title: Double Indemnity
desc: An insurance salesman and a housewife plan to kill her husband. The platonic noir.

title: Sunset Boulevard
desc: A screenwriter becomes trapped by a faded silent film star.

title: Out of the Past
desc: A former private eye is dragged back into his past. Robert Mitchum. The definitive noir.

title: In a Lonely Place
desc: A volatile screenwriter is suspected of murder. Humphrey Bogart's best performance.

title: Laura
desc: A detective investigating a murder falls in love with the victim's portrait.

title: Slow Horses
desc: MI5's dumping ground for failed spies. Gary Oldman as Jackson Lamb. Best spy TV since The Americans.

title: The Wire
desc: The Baltimore drug trade, police, politics, education, press. Five seasons. The standard.

title: The Americans
desc: Soviet spies living as an ordinary American couple. The best spy show ever made.

title: I May Destroy You
desc: A writer processes sexual assault. The most original British TV in a decade.

title: Chernobyl
desc: The 1986 nuclear disaster and cover-up. Five episodes. Almost unbearable. Brilliant.

title: This Is Going to Hurt
desc: A junior doctor in the NHS. Ben Whishaw. Blackly funny and devastating.

title: Detectorists
desc: Two metal detectorists in rural Essex. The most quietly perfect British comedy.

title: Line of Duty
desc: An anti-corruption unit investigates bent coppers. Series 1-5 are unmissable.

title: Fleabag
desc: A woman breaks the fourth wall while her life falls apart. Phoebe Waller-Bridge.

title: Succession
desc: A media dynasty tears itself apart. The defining TV drama of the 2020s.

title: Years and Years
desc: A Manchester family navigates fifteen years of near-future Britain.

title: Shogun
desc: A 16th century English sailor becomes embroiled in Japanese feudal politics.

title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
desc: A painter falls in love with her subject on a remote island. Austere and passionate.

title: The Zone of Interest
desc: The Auschwitz commandant and his family live their normal life adjacent to the camp.

title: All We Imagine as Light
desc: Two nurses in Mumbai. The first Indian film in Cannes competition in 30 years. S&S Film of the Year.

title: Aftersun
desc: A daughter revisits memories of a holiday with her father. Haunting and exquisitely made.

title: The Worst Person in the World
desc: A Norwegian woman in her thirties tries to figure out her life. Funny, sad, real.

title: Drive My Car
desc: A theatre director processes grief while rehearsing Chekhov. Three hours. Unmissable.

title: Tár
desc: A female conductor at the height of her powers faces cancellation.

title: La Chimera
desc: A British archaeologist in 1980s Italy digs for Etruscan tombs with a band of thieves.

title: Nickel Boys
desc: A reform school in Jim Crow Florida. Told entirely in first-person POV.

title: Anora
desc: A sex worker marries a Russian oligarch's son. Screwball turns into something else entirely.

title: Daft Punk Unchained
desc: The full story of Daft Punk's rise. Rare footage and interviews.

title: Krautrock: The Rebirth of Germany
desc: BBC documentary on Can, Kraftwerk, Neu! and the invention of electronic music.

title: Kraftwerk and the Electronic Revolution
desc: The story of Kraftwerk's influence on every strand of modern music.

title: I Dream of Wires
desc: The history of modular synthesizers. Essential for synth obsessives.
The Roland TR-808 drum machine and its influence on popular music.

title: Forbidden Planet
desc: Landmark sci-fi with the first entirely electronic film score by Louis and Bebe Barron.

title: Blade Runner
desc: Vangelis's synthesizer score is as essential as the visuals. A film and a sound.

title: Certified Copy
desc: A writer and a woman debate the nature of copies and originals in Tuscany.

title: Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives
desc: A dying man in a Thai jungle is visited by ghosts. Palme d'Or. Strange and beautiful.

title: The Turin Horse
desc: The last film by Béla Tarr. A horse refuses to move. Seven days until the end of the world.

title: Stories We Tell
desc: Sarah Polley investigates her own family's secrets. Form and content are inseparable.

title: The Favourite
desc: Three women fight for control of Queen Anne's court. Olivia Colman. Vicious and hilarious.

title: Local Hero
desc: An American oil company rep sent to buy a Scottish village falls under its spell. Perfect.

title: Gregory's Girl
desc: A teenage boy falls for the girl who replaced him on the school football team. Glasgow, 1980.

title: Trainspotting
desc: Edinburgh heroin addicts. Danny Boyle at his most visceral. Defined 90s British cinema.

title: Red Road
desc: A CCTV operator in Glasgow becomes obsessed with a man she sees on screen.

title: Ratcatcher
desc: A boy in 1970s Glasgow during the bin-workers' strike. Ramsay's debut. Devastating.

title: Morvern Callar
desc: A supermarket worker in a Highland town submits her dead boyfriend's novel as her own.

title: Sunshine on Leith
desc: Two soldiers return to Edinburgh. Proclaimers jukebox musical. Genuinely moving.

title: Braveheart
desc: William Wallace and the Wars of Scottish Independence. Historically absurd, cinematically overwhelming.

title: Rob Roy
desc: Liam Neeson as Rob Roy MacGregor. Tim Roth's villain is one of the great screen monsters.

title: The Wicker Man
desc: A police sergeant investigates a missing girl on a remote Scottish island. Folk horror. Essential.

title: Restless Natives
desc: Two Edinburgh boys become highway robbers on the tourist trail. Cloven Hoof and Wolfie. Beloved.

title: Whisky Galore!
desc: A ship full of whisky runs aground on a Scottish island during the war. Ealing at its best.

title: The 39 Steps
desc: A man wrongly accused of murder goes on the run across Scotland. Hitchcock's first masterpiece.

title: Shallow Grave
desc: Three Edinburgh flatmates discover their new lodger dead and his suitcase full of cash.

title: I Know Where I'm Going!
desc: A headstrong woman travels to the Hebrides to marry a rich man and gets more than she planned.

title: Aguirre, the Wrath of God
desc: Conquistadors descend the Amazon. Klaus Kinski. Herzog against the jungle. Ebert Great Movie.

title: Ali: Fear Eats the Soul
desc: An elderly German cleaning woman falls in love with a Moroccan mechanic. Fassbinder. Ebert Great Movie.

title: Apocalypse Now
desc: Vietnam. Heart of Darkness. Brando. Sheen. Coppola's madness on screen. Ebert Great Movie.

title: The General
desc: Buster Keaton drives a locomotive through the Civil War. The greatest silent comedy.

title: Ikiru
desc: A bureaucrat learns he is dying and tries to do one good thing. Kurosawa's most human film.

title: Nashville
desc: 24 characters intersect over five days at a country music festival. Altman's masterpiece.

title: Nights of Cabiria
desc: A Roman prostitute searches for love and dignity. Giulietta Masina's greatest performance.

title: The Third Man
desc: Postwar Vienna. Harry Lime. The cuckoo clock speech. The greatest British thriller.

title: Ugetsu
desc: Two peasants abandon their families for glory during civil war and pay the price. Ebert Great Movie.

title: Make Way for Tomorrow
desc: An elderly couple is separated when their children won't take them in. Orson Welles called it the saddest film ever made.

title: His Girl Friday
desc: A newspaper editor tries to win back his ex-wife. The fastest dialogue in Hollywood history.

title: Late Spring
desc: A widower's daughter refuses to leave home. Ozu's most tender film.

title: A Letter to Three Wives
desc: Three women wonder which one of their husbands has run off with a local man-eater.

title: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World
desc: Russell Crowe as Captain Aubrey pursuing a French frigate. Naval warfare, friendship, Darwin. Underseen masterpiece.

title: The Dish
desc: The Australian radio telescope that helped broadcast the Moon landing. Warm, funny, perfect.

title: Galaxy Quest
desc: Washed-up actors from a Star Trek-like show are mistaken for real space heroes. Genuinely great.

title: The Long Goodbye
desc: Elliott Gould as Chandler's Philip Marlowe in 1970s LA. Altman deconstructs the genre lovingly.

title: Withnail and I
desc: Two unemployed actors spend a disastrous holiday in the Lake District. Dialogue you quote forever.

title: Election
desc: Reese Witherspoon vs Matthew Broderick in a high school election. One of the great comedies.

title: Gosford Park
desc: A country house murder mystery. Altman with a British cast. The template for Downton Abbey.
